payload.title: WSL terminal agents need a standard bind-self operator-surface path
payload.summary: An exchange with narada-andrey.builder exposed that WSL/Windows terminal agents still need platform-specific PowerShell instructions to bind their visible window identity. This should be lifted into Narada proper as a standard operator-surface self-identification path.
payload.observation: Current instruction to a WSL terminal agent is: focus your own Windows Terminal window, then run a Windows PowerShell helper such as Show-FocusedWindowIdentityBindingDialog.ps1 or Set-FocusedWindowIdentityBinding.ps1 with an exact identity. This works locally, but it makes the agent/operator remember Windows-specific scripts and exact identity strings. The more coherent primitive is bind-self or bind-focused through Narada's operator-surface machinery, with declared cross-embodiment routing from WSL agent context to PC-local HWND runtime binding.
evidence: Builder advised WSL windows to run powershell.exe -File C:\Users\Andrey\Narada\tools\operator-surface-carriers\Show-FocusedWindowIdentityBindingDialog.ps1 -AssertedBy operator and choose an admitted identity.
Builder also advised the lower-level Set-FocusedWindowIdentityBinding.ps1 -IdentityName <identity-name> path for agents that know their exact identity.
The workflow is correct locally but remains script-path and platform specific.
The missing ergonomic primitive is an agent/window self-identification path: bind this terminal/window to my declared identity without relying on operator memory of exact identity strings.
This is cross-embodiment: WSL agent context, Windows Terminal HWND, PC Site runtime binding, and User Site identity authority.
payload.proposed_direction: Extend the operator-surface identity/binding machinery proposal with commands such as narada operator-surface bind-self, bind-focused --identity <id>, bindings list, unbind-focused, and a UI picker surface. The implementation must preserve the authority split: admitted identities come from Site authority; volatile HWND binding belongs to the PC/runtime locus; WSL-to-Windows invocation uses declared embodiment routing rather than ad hoc PowerShell incantations.
payload.requested_handling: Attach as concrete evidence to the operator-surface binding machinery work, with emphasis on bind-self and cross-embodiment routing.
